narration: Grow the window by adding characters on the right while they stay unique.
callout: The window [a, b, c] is valid because the set has no repeats.
decision: Measure the window when it stays unique.

narration: When a repeated character appears, shrink from the left until the repeat is gone.
callout: The new a repeats, so the left side must move past the old a.
decision: Keep shrinking until the set matches a unique window again.

predictionPrompt.prompt: Why do we use while instead of if when shrinking?

choices.label: Because one move may not remove the repeat
choices.feedback: Correct. The window may need several left moves.

choices.label: Because while is faster than if
choices.feedback: Not this one. The reason is correctness, not speed.

narration: The best answer is the longest window that stays unique after every repeat is cleaned out.
callout: For abca, the best unique window length is still 3.
decision: Return the largest valid window length found.

reflectionPrompt.prompt: Why is a set helpful for this lesson?
